causal_chains.description: Braking too early causes low apex speed
Low apex speed leads to premature throttle application
Aggressive throttle application causes rear instability

zones.template_feedback:
━━━ Zone 1 (54.1% – 57.8% of lap) ━━━
  Severity: ██████████ (100%)
  Estimated time impact: ~0.323s

  Key deviations:
    ▼ RPM: Engine RPM is lower than expected — check your gear selection
      Deviation: 792.5 rpm below expert level

  Root cause analysis:
    → Braking too early causes low apex speed (confidence: 100%)
    💡 Tip: You can brake later and carry more speed into the corner — trust the car's braking capability.
    → Low apex speed leads to premature throttle application (confidence: 70%)
    💡 Tip: Low mid-corner speed is tempting you to get on throttle early. Focus on carrying more apex speed instead.
zones.llm_feedback: You're carrying 14.9 km/h more speed than the expert through this segment, which is a great starting point. However, you're braking 2.3% more than the expert, which is causing you to lose momentum and resulting in a lower apex speed. To improve, try braking 2.3% less and aim to be closer to the expert's braking point, which should allow you to maintain a better speed through the turn. Additionally, you're currently 792.5 rpm below the expert's RPM, so focus on getting back to the optimal gear and RPM range to maximize your speed.

causal_chains.description: Late throttle application compromises corner exit speed
Excessive steering input scrubs speed through the corner

zones.template_feedback:
━━━ Zone 2 (9.1% – 16.7% of lap) ━━━
  Severity: ██████████ (100%)
  Estimated time impact: ~0.120s

  Key deviations:
    ▼ RPM: Engine RPM is lower than expected — check your gear selection
      Deviation: 718.3 rpm below expert level

  Root cause analysis:
    → Late throttle application compromises corner exit speed (confidence: 69%)
    💡 Tip: Get on the throttle earlier and more decisively on corner exit to maximise straight-line speed.
    → Excessive steering input scrubs speed through the corner (confidence: 1%)
    💡 Tip: Reduce steering angle — a straighter wheel lets the car roll faster. Work on your turn-in point.
zones.llm_feedback: You're currently running 718.3 rpm lower than the expert reconstruction, which is likely costing you corner exit speed. To improve, focus on getting on the throttle earlier and more aggressively, aiming to match the expert's RPM. Additionally, try to reduce your steering wheel angle by 1.4 degrees to minimize speed scrub, and aim to brake 0.3% less to maintain momentum. By making these adjustments, you'll be able to carry more speed through the corner and improve your overall lap time.

causal_chains.description: Late throttle application compromises corner exit speed

Additionally, try to reduce your steering wheel angle by 0.6 degrees to be more in line with the expert's input, and aim to brake 2.4% less to find a better balance. By making these adjustments, you'll be able to carry more speed through the corner and improve your overall lap time.

  Overall severity: 100%
  Total estimated time impact: ~0.635s

     Root cause: Braking too early causes low apex speed

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════
summary_llm: The largest area for improvement lies in Zone 1, where braking too early results in a significant loss of apex speed, with the RPM being under by 792.5 rpm. To address this, focus on delaying your braking point to increase your apex speed, aiming to reduce the RPM deficit in this zone. Despite the estimated total time loss of ~0.635s, it's encouraging to see that the issues identified are largely related to throttle application and braking timing, which can be refined with practice. By making adjustments to your braking and throttle technique, you can work towards shaving off precious seconds from your lap time.
